channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump extends ceasefire deadline with Iran
item.description: President Trump again backs down ahead of a critical ceasefire deadline with Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump again backs down ahead of a critical ceasefire deadline with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Former ambassador on Trump's decision to extend the ceasefire with Iran
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Ambassador Ryan Crocker, who served in a number of countries across the Middle East, about President Trump's decision to extend the ceasefire with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Ambassador Ryan Crocker, who served in a number of countries across the Middle East, about President Trump's decision to extend the ceasefire with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries says Virginia redistricting assures a 'free and fair midterm'
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries says Trump's effort to try to rig the midterm elections has been "thwarted," after a major victory for Democrats in Virginia.
item.content:encoded: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries says Trump's effort to try to rig the midterm elections has been "thwarted," after a major victory for Democrats in Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: 'We can do better,' FAA head says of work to update U.S. analog air traffic system
item.description: The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump extends ceasefire deadline with Iran, Virginians approve Democratic effort to redraw congressional map, Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh vows to protect Fed's independence in confirmation hearing.
item.content:encoded: Trump extends ceasefire deadline with Iran, Virginians approve Democratic effort to redraw congressional map, Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh vows to protect Fed's independence in confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: 'Here Where We Live Is Our Country' tells the story of a Jewish labor movement
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with writer and artist Molly Crabapple about her new book, "Here Where We Live Is Our Country."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with writer and artist Molly Crabapple about her new book, "Here Where We Live Is Our Country."
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Florida Democrat Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, facing possible expulsion, resigns
item.description: Florida Democrat Sheila Chefilus-McCormick announced on Tuesday she will step down from Congress, becoming the third House member to resign this month.
item.content:encoded: Florida Democrat Sheila Chefilus-McCormick announced on Tuesday she will step down from Congress, becoming the third House member to resign this month.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: The little-known DOJ division turning Trump's immigration policies into binding law
item.description: A little-known division inside the Justice Department that reviews immigration court appeals is turning President Trump's immigration policy agenda into law.
item.content:encoded: A little-known division inside the Justice Department that reviews immigration court appeals is turning President Trump's immigration policy agenda into law.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: DOJ indicts Southern Poverty Law Center on federal fraud charges
item.description: The Justice Department indicted the Southern Poverty Law Center Tuesday, alleging the civil rights group defrauded donors by using their money to pay informants to infiltrate extremist groups.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department indicted the Southern Poverty Law Center Tuesday, alleging the civil rights group defrauded donors by using their money to pay informants to infiltrate extremist groups.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Warehouse backlog leaves Mississippi's alcohol supply high and dry
item.description: A backlog at the only alcohol warehouse in Mississippi is causing liquor stores to run out of products to sell.
item.content:encoded: A backlog at the only alcohol warehouse in Mississippi is causing liquor stores to run out of products to sell.
item.dc:creator: Shamira Muhammad
